SEARCH
Package fr.sorbonne_u.cps.pubsub.base.components

Class AbstractPluginComponent

java.lang.Object
fr.sorbonne_u.components.AbstractComponent
fr.sorbonne_u.cps.pubsub.base.components.AbstractPluginComponent
All Implemented Interfaces:
fr.sorbonne_u.components.ComponentI
public abstract class AbstractPluginComponent extends fr.sorbonne_u.components.AbstractComponent
opt-in abstract base for clients composed from the four pub/sub plugins (ClientRegistrationPlugin, ClientSubscriptionPlugin, ClientPublicationPlugin, ClientPrivilegedPlugin).

This class exists alongside PluginClient, which is kept concrete because several existing demos and the application/meteo layer instantiate it directly (or extend it via ScenarioPluginClient). New components that want to demonstrate clean plugin composition (for example dedicated SubscriberClient / PublisherClient variants) should extend AbstractPluginComponent instead, override scenarioExecute() with the role-specific behaviour, and pass an RegistrationCI.RegistrationClass at construction time so that the lifecycle template registers them automatically.

Lifecycle template

  1. The constructor installs the four plugins and configures the reception callback to onReceive(String, MessageI) (which subclasses may override).
  2. BCM4Java calls execute(); this method is final and follows the convention "register in execute(), never in start() or constructors":
    • super.execute() runs first;
    • if an initialRC was supplied, the registration plugin's register(initialRC) is invoked;
    • then scenarioExecute() is called, where subclasses drive the actual scenario (subscribe / publish / privileged channel management).

Subclasses that need additional plugins should install them in their own constructor after calling super(...); they will be live before execute() runs.

  • Method Details

    • execute

      public final void execute() throws Exception
      Final lifecycle template — see class javadoc for the ordered steps. Subclasses override scenarioExecute() instead of this method.
      Specified by:
      execute in interface fr.sorbonne_u.components.ComponentI
      Overrides:
      execute in class fr.sorbonne_u.components.AbstractComponent
      Throws:
      Exception

    • scenarioExecute

      protected void scenarioExecute() throws Exception
      Subclasses implement the actual scenario here (subscribe, publish, privileged-channel actions, optional accelerated-clock test scenario, etc.). The default implementation does nothing so that subclasses with no scenario logic still compile.
      Throws:
      Exception - any error from the scenario logic.

    • getReceptionPortURI

      public String getReceptionPortURI()
      URI of this client's ReceivingCI inbound port.

    • onReceive

      public void onReceive(String channel, MessageI message)
      Default reception callback wired into the subscription plugin. Subclasses can override to implement role-specific message handling (e.g. enqueue, log, forward to another component).
